IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: 4th Watch Educational Services, Inc., operating as Machinist Career College (MCC), is a nonprofit workforce development organization focused on preparing individuals for high-demand careers in precision manufacturing. MCC delivers industry-aligned training programs designed to create pathways to sustainable, living-wage employment for underserved populations, including low-income adults, veterans, and justice-impacted individuals.
IRS990.MissionDesc: 4th Watch Educational Services, Inc., operating as Machinist Career College (MCC), is a nonprofit workforce development organization focused on preparing individuals for high-demand careers in precision manufacturing. MCC delivers industry-aligned training programs designed to create pathways to sustainable, living-wage employment for underserved populations, including low-income adults, veterans, and justice-impacted individuals.
IRS990.Desc: The organization operated a registered apprenticeship program in precision machining, providing hands-on training in CNC operations, blueprint reading, and Mastercam programming. The program served adult learners seeking entry into the manufacturing workforce and was delivered in partnership with employer organizations to support job placement and career advancement.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: The organization operated a registered apprenticeship program in precision machining, providing hands-on training in CNC operations, blueprint reading, and Mastercam programming. The program served adult learners seeking entry into the manufacturing workforce and was delivered in partnership with employer organizations to support job placement and career advancement.

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: The organization conducts fundraising activities through charitable contributions from individuals, foundations, and corporate partners. No professional fundraising services were utilized during the tax year.

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part III, Line 12 Other Program Service Revenue
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: Other program service revenue consists of training-related income associated with workforce development programs, including employer-supported training and program participation fees

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part IV, Section A Governance and Policies
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: The organization maintains written governance policies, including a conflict of interest policy, whistleblower policy, and document retention and destruction policy. These policies are reviewed and monitored by the organizations leadership and governing body.

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part IV, Section B Books and Records
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: The organizations books and records are maintained at its principal office and are under the supervision of the organizations management.

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part IV, Section C Public Disclosure
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: The organization makes its Form 990, governing documents, conflict of interest policy, and financial statements available to the public upon request at its principal office.
The organization files all required information returns in accordance with applicable Internal Revenue Service requirements.

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part V, Section B, Line 1e Tax Compliance
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: The organization has complied with applicable federal tax reporting requirements during the tax year.

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part V, Section D Foreign Activities
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: The organization does not conduct activities outside of the United States and does not maintain foreign financial accounts or investments.

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part V, Section E Other Information
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: The organization operates as a workforce development training provider focused on delivering technical education and apprenticeship-related instruction aligned with industry demand. The organization prepares its financial records using the accrual method of accounting. The organization is in the process of engaging an independent accounting firm to complete audited financial statements for the tax year.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The organization did not conduct fundraising events during the tax year.
The organization did not conduct gaming activities during the tax year.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part II, Line 9 Fundraising Professional Fees
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The organization did not engage professional fundraising services during the tax year.

4th Watch Educational Services, Inc., operating as Machinist Career College (MCC), provides workforce development training in precision manufacturing and CNC machining. The organization delivers apprenticeship programs, technical training, and career readiness services designed to prepare individuals for employment in high-demand industries.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part III, Line 4 Program Service Accomplishments
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The organization provides hands-on training programs in machining, CNC operations, and manufacturing technology. Programs are delivered through structured coursework, practical shop experience, and industry-aligned curriculum to support workforce entry and advancement for adult learners, including underserved populations.
The organization did not engage in lobbying activities during the tax year.
The organization did not engage in political campaign activities during the tax year.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part V, Line 4 Foreign Bank Accounts
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The organization did not maintain foreign bank accounts or financial accounts outside of the United States.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part X, Line 2 Liability for Uncertain Tax Positions
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The organization did not have audited financial statements and therefore does not have a footnote related to uncertain tax positions under FASB ASC 740.
No amounts were reported for other adjustments.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part XI, Line 4b Other Changes in Net Assets
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The organization recorded prior period adjustments and opening equity balances during the tax year related to the consolidation and alignment of financial records. These amounts are reflected as other changes in net assets.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part XII, Line 2d Other Accounting Information
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: No additional accounting method disclosures are required.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part XII, Line 4b Changes in Accounting Policies
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The organization did not change its method of accounting during the tax year. The organization prepares its financial records using the accrual method of accounting. The organization is in the process of engaging an independent accounting firm to complete audited financial statements for the tax year.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part I, Line 3 Nondiscrimination Policy Publication
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The organization publicizes its racially nondiscriminatory policy through its website, student catalog, and enrollment materials. These materials are made available to prospective students and the general public as part of the organizations admissions and outreach process.

The organization maintains records of student enrollment, program participation, and administrative operations, including copies of catalogs, brochures, and other written communications related to admissions and programs. Records are maintained in the ordinary course of operations.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part I, Line 5h Scholarships and Financial Assistance
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The organization administers financial assistance and program participation opportunities in a nondiscriminatory manner based on eligibility criteria and program requirements.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part I, Line 6b Public Disclosure of Policy
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The organizations nondiscrimination policy is communicated through publicly available materials, including its website and student-facing documents, ensuring that the policy is accessible to all segments of the community it serves.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part I, Line 7 Compliance with Rev. Proc. 75-50
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The organization certifies that it has complied with the applicable requirements of Revenue Procedure 75-50 regarding racial nondiscrimination. The organization does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, or national or ethnic origin in the administration of its educational policies, admissions policies, or other school-administered programs. The organization operates as a workforce development training provider offering technical education and apprenticeship-related instruction. Programs are open to eligible participants without regard to race, color, or national or ethnic origin.
The Form 990 was prepared by management and reviewed by the organizations governing body prior to filing.

The organization maintains a written conflict of interest policy that requires annual disclosure by members of the Board of Directors and key employees. Any potential conflicts are reviewed by the Board, and individuals with a conflict are required to recuse themselves from discussions and decisions related to the matter. Compliance with the policy is monitored by the organizations leadership and governing body on an ongoing basis.

The Board of Directors reviewed and approved compensation for executive leadership based on consideration of comparable data, the scope of responsibilities, and the organizations operational needs. Compensation decisions were documented and approved by independent members of the governing body.

In addition to the officers and directors listed, the organization may designate certain individuals as key employees based on their roles and responsibilities in supporting program operations and organizational functions. These individuals do not serve as members of the governing body and do not have voting authority. Compensation and responsibilities for such positions are determined based on operational needs and are reviewed as part of the organizations overall governance and management processes.

The organization makes its governing documents, conflict of interest policy, and financial statements available to the public upon request. Requests may be made to the organization's principal office, and documents are provided in a timely manner in accordance with applicable requirements.

The organization recorded prior period adjustments and opening equity balances in 2025 related to the transition and consolidation of financial records. These adjustments are reflected as other changes in net assets.
The organization prepares its financial records using the accrual method of accounting.
The organization is in the process of engaging an independent accounting firm to complete audited financial statements for the year.
